verifyNote: Immigration rules change frequently. Confirm current rules on uscis.gov and with an immigration attorney for your own situation.
filingWindowNote: USCIS generally lets you file an I-765 renewal up to 180 days before your current EAD expires. File as early in this window as possible.

autoExtension.summary: A DHS Interim Final Rule effective October 30, 2025 removed the automatic extension of EADs on timely-filed renewals.

preRule.appliesIf: I-765 renewal RECEIVED before 2025-10-30 (receipt date on Form I-797C)
preRule.capNote: Extension ran to the EARLIER of 540 days from the EAD 'Card Expires' date OR the end date of your H-4 I-94. It also required staying in valid H-4 status (unexpired I-94).

postRule.note: No automatic extension. When the EAD expires you must STOP working until the new EAD is approved and received.

processing.postedVsActualNote: Processing time depends on the form category and the USCIS office or service center handling your case, and it changes month to month. Check your own case category and location on the official USCIS processing-times tool rather than relying on a general range. You usually cannot submit a case inquiry until your case is outside the posted time for your form and office.

premiumProcessing.note: Premium processing (Form I-907) is NOT available for H-4 EAD category (c)(26). For Form I-765, USCIS limits premium processing to F-1 student categories (c)(3)(A), (c)(3)(B) and (c)(3)(C) requesting OPT or a STEM OPT extension. An I-907 filed for a (c)(26) application will be rejected. There is no way to pay to speed up an H-4 EAD.
premiumProcessing.sourceLabel: USCIS — How Do I Request Premium Processing?

concurrentFiling.note: You can file the H-4 extension (I-539) and H-4 EAD (I-765) together with the H-1B I-129. USCIS is not required to adjudicate them together, so the EAD can lag the H-1B approval by many months.

dependencyRisk: An H-4 EAD is valid only while the H-4 status is valid, which depends on the H-1B spouse maintaining H-1B status. If the H-1B lapses (job loss, status issue), the H-4 and the EAD — and any job or business run on it — are affected.
